subject: Mathematics
q: The value of $\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}$ is
question: The value of $\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}$ is
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}=\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)} \\
& =\sqrt{2} \int\left(\cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \sin \frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x \\
& =\int d x+\int \cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x \\
& =x+\ln \left|\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right|+c .
\end{aligned}
$$

q: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>8</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>dx</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>12</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to :
question: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>8</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>dx</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>12</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to :
solution:
Let, I=∫x8-x2x12+3x6+1tan-1x3+1x3dx

Putting, tan-1x3+1x3=t

⇒11+x3+1x32·3x2-3x4dx=dt

⇒11+x6+1x6+2·3x2-3x4dx=dt

⇒x6x12+3x6+1·3x6-3x4dx=dt

⇒x8-x2x12+3x6+1dx=dt3

⇒I=13∫1tdt

⇒I=13log|t|+C

⇒I=13logtan-1x3+1x3+C

⇒I=logtan-1x3+1x313+C

Hence optin A is correct

q: The value of the integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>·</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn></msqrt></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>θ</mi></math> is (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is a constant of integration)

solution:
I=∫sinθ·sin2θsin6θ+sin4θ+sin2θ2sin4θ+3sin2θ+61-cos2θdθ

⇒ I=∫sinθ.2sinθcosθ·sin2θsin4θ+sin2θ+12sin4θ+3sin2θ+61/22sin2θdθ

=∫sin2θ·cosθsin4θ+sin2θ+12sin4θ+3sin2θ+61/2 dθ

Let sinθ=t⇒cosθdθ=dt

∴ I=∫t2t4+t2+12t4+3t2+61/2dt

=∫t5+t3+tt2t4+3t2+61/2dt

=∫t5+t3+tt21/22t4+3t2+61/2dt

=∫t5+t3+t2t6+3t4+6t21/2dt

Let 2t6+3t4+6t2=u2

⇒ 12t5+t3+tdt=2udu

∴ I=∫u21/2·2udu12

=∫u26 du=u318+C

=2t6+3t4+6t23/218+C

when t=sinθ

and t2=1-cos2θ will give

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\int x^3 \sin x \mathrm{~d} x=g(x)+C$, where $C$ is the constant of integration. If $8\left(g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)+g^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)=\alpha \pi^3+\beta \pi^2+\gamma, \alpha, \beta, \gamma \in Z$, then $\alpha+\beta-\gamma$ equals :
question: Let $\int x^3 \sin x \mathrm{~d} x=g(x)+C$, where $C$ is the constant of integration. If $8\left(g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)+g^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)=\alpha \pi^3+\beta \pi^2+\gamma, \alpha, \beta, \gamma \in Z$, then $\alpha+\beta-\gamma$ equals :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \int x^3 \sin x d x=-x^3 \cos x+\int 3 x^2 \cos x d x \\
& =-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x-\int 6 x \sin x d x \\
& =-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x+6 x \cos x-6 \sin x+c
\end{aligned}$

So $g(x)=-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x+6 x \cos x-6 \sin x$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{3 \pi^2}{4}-6 \\
& \mathrm{~g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=-3 \mathrm{x}^2 \cos \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}^3 \sin \mathrm{x}+6 \cos \mathrm{x}-6 \cos \mathrm{x} \\
& \mathrm{~g}^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi^3}{8} \\
& 8\left(\mathrm{~g}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)+\mathrm{g}^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)=\pi^3+6 \pi^2-48
\end{aligned}$

So $\alpha+\beta-\gamma=55$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $f(x)=\int \frac{\left(2-x^{2}\right) \cdot \mathrm{e}^{x}}{(\sqrt{1+x})(1-x)^{3 / 2}} \mathrm{~d} x$. If $f(0)=0$, then $f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ is equal to:
question: Let $f(x)=\int \frac{\left(2-x^{2}\right) \cdot \mathrm{e}^{x}}{(\sqrt{1+x})(1-x)^{3 / 2}} \mathrm{~d} x$. If $f(0)=0$, then $f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ is equal to:
solution:
Write $2 - x^2 = (1-x)(1+x) + 1$, so the integrand splits as $e^x\left(\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}\right)$.
Let $g(x) = \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}}$. Then $g'(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}$.
Using $\int e^x(g + g')dx = e^x g + C$: $f(x) = e^x\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + C$.
$f(0) = 1 + C = 0 \Rightarrow C = -1$.
$f(1/2) = e^{1/2}\sqrt{\frac{3/2}{1/2}} - 1 = \sqrt{e}\cdot\sqrt{3} - 1 = \sqrt{3e} - 1$.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\int \frac{2-\tan x}{3+\tan x} \mathrm{~d} x=\frac{1}{2}\left(\alpha x+\log _{\mathrm{e}}|\beta \sin x+\gamma \cos x|\right)+C$, where $C$ is the constant of integration. Then $\alpha+\frac{\gamma}{\beta}$ is equal to :
question: Let $\int \frac{2-\tan x}{3+\tan x} \mathrm{~d} x=\frac{1}{2}\left(\alpha x+\log _{\mathrm{e}}|\beta \sin x+\gamma \cos x|\right)+C$, where $C$ is the constant of integration. Then $\alpha+\frac{\gamma}{\beta}$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{2-\tan x}{3+\tan x} d x=\int \frac{2 \cos x-\sin x}{3 \cos x+\sin x} d x \\ & 2 \cos x-\sin x=A(3 \cos x+\sin x)+B(\cos x-3 \sin x) \\ & 3 A+B=2 \\ & A-3 B=-1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow A=\frac{1}{2}, B=\frac{1}{2} \\ & \therefore \int \frac{2 \cos x-\sin x}{3 \cos x+\sin x} d x \\ & =\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \ln |3 \cos x+\sin x|+C \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(x+\ln |3 \cos x+\sin x|)+C \\ & =\frac{1}{2}(\alpha x+\ln |\beta \sin x+\gamma \cos x|)+C \\ & \alpha=1, \beta=1, \gamma=3 \\ & \therefore \alpha+\frac{\gamma}{\beta}=1+\frac{3}{1}=4\end{aligned}$

=∫sin2xcos2xsin3xsin2x+cos2x+cos3x cos2x+sin2x2dx

∫etcost dt=12etcost+sint+C, where C is the constant of integration.

solution:
We have, I=∫sin5x2sinx2dx  

=∫sin2x+x2sinx2dx

Using sinA+B=sinAcosB+cosAsinB

=∫sin2xcosx2+cos2xsinx2sinx2dx

=∫2 sinx cosxcosx2sinx2+cos 2xdx

=∫22sinx2cosx2cosx cosx2sinx2+cos 2xdx

Using  cos2x=2cos2x-1⇒2cos2x=1+cos2x

=∫2cosx+2cos2x+cos2xdx

=2sinx+sin2x+x+c, where c is the constant of integration.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>log</mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> , then the ordered pair <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>A</mi><mi>log</mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> , then the ordered pair <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given f3x-43x+4=x+2, x≠-43

Let,  3x-43x+4=t

3x-4=3tx+4t

x=4t+43-3t+2

∴ft=10-2t3-3t

⇒fx=2x-103x-3

∫fxdx=∫2x-103x-3dx

=∫2x3x-3dx-10∫dx3x-3

=2x3-83lnx-1+C, where C is the constant of integration.

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\int \frac{1}{\sqrt[5]{(x-1)^4(x+3)^6}} \mathrm{~d} x=\mathrm{A}\left(\frac{\alpha x-1}{\beta x+3}\right)^B+\mathrm{C}$, where $\mathrm{C}$ is the constant of integration, then the value of $\alpha+\beta+20 \mathrm{AB}$ is__________
question: If $\int \frac{1}{\sqrt[5]{(x-1)^4(x+3)^6}} \mathrm{~d} x=\mathrm{A}\left(\frac{\alpha x-1}{\beta x+3}\right)^B+\mathrm{C}$, where $\mathrm{C}$ is the constant of integration, then the value of $\alpha+\beta+20 \mathrm{AB}$ is__________
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{1}{\sqrt[5]{(x-1)^4(x+3)^6}} d x=A\left(\frac{\alpha x-1}{\beta x+3}\right)^B+C \\ & I=\int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{4 / 5}(x+3)^{6 / 5}} d x \\ & I=\int \frac{1}{\left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)^{4 / 5}(x+3)^2} d x \\ & \left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)=t \Rightarrow \frac{4}{(x+3)^2} d x=d t \quad t^{-4 / 5+1} \\ & I=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{t^{4 / 5}} d t=\frac{1}{4} \frac{t^{1 / 5}}{1 / 5}+c \\ & I=\frac{5}{4}\left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)^{1 / 5}+C \\ & A=\frac{5}{4} \quad \alpha=\beta=1 \quad B=\frac{1}{5} \\ & \alpha+\beta+20 A B=2+20 \times \frac{5}{4} \times \frac{1}{5}=7\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> where <math><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced open="|" close="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></math> where <math><mi>C</mi></math> is a constant of integration, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>θ</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
∫sec2θ1+tan2θ1-tan2θ+2tanθ1-tan2θdθ

=∫sec2θ1-tan2θ1+tanθ2dθ

=∫sec2θ1-tanθ1+tanθdθ

Let tanθ=t⇒sec2θdθ=dt

=∫1-t1+tdt=∫-1+21+tdt

=-t+2ln1+t+C

=-tanθ+2ln1+tanθ+C

⇒λ=-1 and fθ=1+tanθ

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><msqrt><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo><</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfrac><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to _______.
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><msqrt><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo><</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfrac><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to _______.
solution:
Given,

fx=∫dx3+4x24-3x2

Put x=1t, dx=-1t2dt

So, fx=∫-dtt23+4t24-3t2

⇒fx=∫-t dt3t2+44t2-3

Now let, 4t2-3=λ2⇒8t dt=2λ dλ

⇒fx=-∫λ dλ4·3λ2+34+4·λ

⇒fx=-∫dλ3λ2+9+16

⇒fx=-∫dλ3λ2+25

⇒fx=-13∫dλλ2+253

⇒fx=-13×35tan-13λ5+c

⇒fx=-315tan-134-3x25x+c

Now using, f0=0⇒c=+3π30

Hence, f1=-315tan-135+315×π2

⇒f1=-315tan-135-π2

⇒f1=315π2-tan-135

⇒f1=315cot-135

⇒f1=315tan-153

⇒f1=153tan-153

Now comparing with given value of f1 we get, α=5 & β=3

⇒α2+β2=28

solution:
fx=∫x1+x2dx

Let x=tan2θ

dx=2tanθsec2θ dθ

fx=∫tanθ1+tan2θ2.2tanθsec2θ dθ

fx=∫tanθsec4θ.2tanθsec2θ dθ

fx=∫2tan2θ.cos2θ dθ

fx=∫2sin2θ dθ

fx=∫1−cos2θ dθ

fx=θ−sin2θ2+C=θ−tanθ1+tan2θ+C

fx=tan−1x−x1+x+C

Now, f3−f1=tan−3−31+3−tan−11+11+1

=π3−π4+12−34=π12+12-34

q: $\int \frac{d x}{\cos x-\sin x}$ is equal to
question: $\int \frac{d x}{\cos x-\sin x}$ is equal to
solution: $\int \frac{d x}{\cos x-\sin x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \frac{1}{\cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)} d x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \log \left|\tan \left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{3 \pi}{8}\right)\right|+C$

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given that Ix=∫x2x sec2x+tanx(x tanx+1)2dx

Apply Integration by parts.

∫fxgxdx=fx∫gxdx-∫f'x∫gxdxdx

=x2∫x sec2x+tanx(x tanx+1)2dx-∫dx2dx∫x sec2x+tanx(x tanx+1)2dxdx

Let xtanx+1=p

⇒x sec2x+tanxdp=dx

=x2∫dpp2-∫2x∫dpp2dx

=-x2(x tanx+1)+∫2xx tanx+1dx

Let I1=2∫xx tanx+1dx

=2∫x cosxx sinx+cosxdx

Let x sinx+cosx=t

⇒(x cosx+sinx-sinx)dx=dt

⇒(x cosx)dx=dt

=2∫dtt=2logt+c

=2log|x sinx+cosx|+c

∴∫x2xsec2x+tanx(xtanx+1)2dx

=-x2x tanx+1+2log|x sinx+cosx|+c

But I(0)=0

⇒c=0

Also,Iπ4=-π42π4×1+1+2log12π4+12

=loge(π+4)232-π24(π+4)

Hence, this is the correct option.

q: If $f(x)=\int \frac{1}{x^{1 / 4}\left(1+x^{1 / 4}\right)} \mathrm{d} x, f(0)=-6$, then $f(1)$ is equal to :
question: If $f(x)=\int \frac{1}{x^{1 / 4}\left(1+x^{1 / 4}\right)} \mathrm{d} x, f(0)=-6$, then $f(1)$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Put } x^{1 / 4}=t \Rightarrow d x=4 t^3 d t \\ & \int \frac{4 t^3 d t}{t(t+1)}=4 \int\left(\frac{t^2-1}{t+1}+\frac{1}{t+1}\right) d t \\ & f(x)=4\left[\frac{x^{1 / 2}}{2}-x^{1 / 4}+\ln \left|x^{1 / 4}+1\right|\right]+C\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & f(0)=-6 \\ & \Rightarrow C=-6 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad f(1)=4\left(\log _e 2-2\right)\end{aligned}$

Given,

I=∫x2x+2xxlog2x dx

Now, Let x2x=t

⇒  xlog2x2=log2t

⇒  xlogex2·log2e=loget·log2e

On differentiating both sides we get :
logex2+x·2x·12=1tdtdx

⇒logex2+1=1tdtdx

Then solution is not possible as there is no proper substitution.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{\sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x}{\left(\sin ^3 x+\cos ^3 x\right)^2} d x$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\mathrm{I}=\int\left(\frac{\sin x \cdot \cos x}{\sin ^3 x+\cos ^3 x}\right)^2 d x \\
&\mathrm{I}=\int\left(\frac{\sin x \cdot \cos x}{\cos ^3 x\left(1+\tan ^3 x\right)}\right)^2 d x \\
&=\int\left(\frac{\sin x \cdot \sec ^2 x}{\left(1+\tan ^3 x\right)}\right)^2 d x \\
&\text { Put } 1+\tan ^3 x=\mathrm{t} \\
&d t=3 \tan ^2 x \sec ^2 x d x \text { or } d x \\
&=\frac{d t}{3 \tan ^2 x \sec ^2 x} \\
&\therefore \mathrm{I}=\int \frac{\sin ^2 x \cdot \sec ^4 x}{t^2} \times \frac{d t}{3 \tan ^2 x \sec ^2 x}
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
&\mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{\sin ^2 x \cdot \sec ^4 x}{t^2} \times \frac{d t}{\frac{\sin ^2 x}{\cos ^2 x} \times \sec ^2 x} \\
&=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{\sin ^2 x \cdot \sec ^4 x}{t^2} \times \frac{d t}{\sin ^2 x \sec ^4 x} \\
&\therefore \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{d t}{t^2}=\frac{1}{3} \int t^{-2} d t \\
&\mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{t^{-2+1}}{-2+1}\right]+c=\frac{-1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{t}\right]+c \\
&\text { or } \mathrm{I}=-\frac{1}{3\left(1+\tan ^3 x\right)}+c
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: If $f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x$ and $f(0)=0$, then $f(1)$ equals
question: If $f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x$ and $f(0)=0$, then $f(1)$ equals
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\frac{\sin ^2 x}{\cos ^2 x}}{1+x^2} d x
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+\tan ^2 x\left(1+x^2\right)}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2}{1+x^2} d x+\int \tan ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+1-1}{1+x^2} \mathrm{dx}+\int\left(\sec ^2 \mathrm{x}-1 \mathrm{dx}\right. \\
& =\int 1 d x-\int \frac{d x}{1+x^2}+\int \sec ^2 x d x-\int d x \\
& =-\tan -1 x+\tan ^{-1} x+\mathrm{c} \\
& \text { Given: } f(0)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow f(0)=-\tan ^{-1} 0+\tan ^2+c \\
& \Rightarrow c=0 \\
& \therefore f(x)=-\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^2 x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now,
$$
f(1)=-\tan ^{-1}(1)+\tan 1=\tan 1-\frac{\pi}{4}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral of $\frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1}$ w.r.t. $x$ is
question: The integral of $\frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1}$ w.r.t. $x$ is
solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{x^2-x}{x^3-x^2+x-1} d x$
$$
=\int \frac{x(x-1)}{x^2(x-1)+(x-1)} d x=\int \frac{x d x}{x^2+1}
$$

$$
\left.=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x d x}{\left(x^2+1\right.}\right)
$$
Let $x^2+1=t \Rightarrow 2 x d x=d t$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{2} \log t+c \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^2+1\right)+c
\end{aligned}
$$
where ' $c$ ' is the constant of integration.

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
question: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
solution:
I=∫dx1+xx1-x

Put, 1+x=t ⇒12xdx=dt

And, 1-x=1-t-12=2t-t2

∴ I= ∫2dtt2t-t2

Again put, t=1z⇒dt=-1z2dz

⇒I=2∫-1z2 dz1z 2z-1z2=2∫-dz2z-1=-22z-1+c

Where c is arbitrary constant.

=-22t-1 +c

=-22-tt+c

=-2 1-x1+x+c

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $f(x)$ be an indefinite integral of $\cos ^3 x$.
Statement 1: $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period
$\pi$.
Statement 2: $\cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function.
question:
Let $f(x)$ be an indefinite integral of $\cos ^3 x$.
Statement 1: $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period
$\pi$.
Statement 2: $\cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function.
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
Both the Statements are true, but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Both the Statements are true, and Statement 2 is correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
solution:
Statement $-2: \cos ^3 x$ is a periodic function. it is a true statement.
Statement-1
Given $f(x)=\int \cos ^3 x d x$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\int\left(\frac{\cos 3 x}{4}+\frac{3 \cos x}{4}\right) d x \\
& =\frac{1}{4} \frac{\sin 3 x}{3}+\frac{3}{4} \sin x \\
& =\frac{1}{12} \sin 3 x+\frac{3}{4} \sin x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, period of $\frac{1}{12} \sin 3 x=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$
Period of $\frac{3}{4} \sin x=2 \pi$
Hence period of $f(x)=\frac{\text { L.C.M. }(2 \pi, 2 \pi}{\text { HCF of }(1,3)}$

$$
=\frac{2 \pi}{1}=2 \pi
$$
Thus, $f(x)$ is a periodic function of period $2 \pi$.
Hence, Statement $-1$ is false.

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x=A(x) e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+C$, then $A(x)$ is equal to :
question: If $\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x=A(x) e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+C$, then $A(x)$ is equal to :
solution:
Let $\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{x^2-x+1}{x^2+1} \cdot e^{\cot ^{-1} x} d x$
Put $x=\cot t \Rightarrow-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t d t=d x$
Now, $1+\cot ^2 t=\operatorname{cosec}^2 t$
$\therefore$
$\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{e^t\left(\cot ^2 t-\cot t+1\right)}{\left(1+\cot ^2 t\right)}\left(-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t\right) d t$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =-\int e^t\left(\operatorname{cosec}{ }^2 t-\cot t\right) d t \\
& =\int e^t\left(\cot t-\operatorname{cosec}^2 t\right) d t \\
& =e^t \cot t+\mathrm{C} \\
& =e^{\cot ^{-1} x}(x)+\mathrm{C} \equiv \mathrm{A}(x) \cdot e^{\cot ^{-1} x}+\mathrm{C} \\
\Rightarrow \mathrm{A}(x) & =x
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{I}(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}}$. If $\mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~b}^{\frac{1}{13}}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}^{\frac{1}{13}}}\right), \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})$ is equal to
question: Let $\mathrm{I}(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}}$. If $\mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~b}^{\frac{1}{13}}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}^{\frac{1}{13}}}\right), \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & I(x)=\int \frac{d x}{(x-11)^{\frac{11}{13}}(x+15)^{\frac{15}{13}}} \\ & \text { Put } \frac{x-11}{x+15}=t \Rightarrow \frac{26}{(x+5)^2} d x=d t \\ & I(x)=\frac{1}{26} \int \frac{d t}{t^{1 / 13}}=\frac{1}{26} \cdot \frac{t^{2 / 13}}{2 / 13}\end{aligned}$


$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-11}{\mathrm{x}+15}\right)^{2 / 13}+\mathrm{C} \\ & \mathrm{I}(37)-\mathrm{I}(24)=\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{26}{52}\right)^{2 / 13}-\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{13}{39}\right)^{2 / 13} \\ & =\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{2^{2 / 13}}-\frac{1}{3^{2 / 13}}\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{1}{4^{1 / 13}}-\frac{1}{9^{1 / 13}}\right) \\ & \therefore \mathrm{b}=4, \mathrm{c}=9 \\ & 3(\mathrm{~b}+\mathrm{c})=39\end{aligned}$
